name: mastermind-ai

metadata.name: mastermind-ai
metadata.summary: A Python package that simulates the Mastermind game with an AI solver.

License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/FlysonBot/Mastermind)](https://github.com/FlysonBot/Mastermind/blob/main/LICENSE) | > [!NOTE] > This repo is currently still under development. Currently there is a beta version that have the basic simulation feature finished. If you encountered any issue, please open up an issue and let me know! I will try to fix them as soon as possible. **Links:** - [Documentation](https://flysonbot.github.io/Mastermind/) - [Source Code](https://github.com/FlysonBot/Mastermind) - [Releases](https://github.com/FlysonBot/Mastermind/releases) - [Bug Reports](https://github.com/FlysonBot/Mastermind/issues) - [Changelog](https://github.com/FlysonBot/Mastermind/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) - [Contributing](https://github.com/FlysonBot/Mastermind/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) - [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/FlysonBot/Mastermind/blob/main/.github/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) # Mastermind > This is a python implementation of the classic puzzle game Mastermind. It simulates the game and allow you to play with either another human being (sits next to you) or the computer, with a AI Solver build-in (still under development). You can install this game with pip or try it out in your browser with [Google Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/FlysonBot/Mastermind/blob/main/examples/mastermind_in_colab.ipynb). ## What is Mastermind? Mastermind is a code-breaking game for two players. The first player (the code-setter) creates a secret code, which the second player (the code-cracker) tries to guess. The code-cracker has a limited number of attempts to guess the code correctly. After each guess, the code-setter provides feedback to the code-cracker, indicating how many dots have the right color and are in the right place, and how many are the right color but in the wrong place. The code-cracker uses this feedback to refine their guesses until they correctly guess the code or run out of attempts. ## Getting Started ### Prerequisites To run this project, you must have the following installed (installation guide below): - Python 3.10 (or higher) - pip (comes with Python, needed to install the project as a library) Or alternatively you can run this program in your browser with [Google Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/FlysonBot/Mastermind/blob/main/mastermind_in_colab.ipynb) ### Installation Guide 1. Install [Python 3.10+](https://www.python.org/downloads/) if you have not already. 2. Install this the latest python release using pip in your terminal: ```bash pip install mastermind-ai ``` 3. Run the program with the following command: ```bash mastermind ``` 4. Enjoy! > [!TIP] > If the above does not work, try the troubleshooting guide below. ### Troubleshooting If you encounter any issues during installation, please check the following: 1. Do you have trouble finding your terminal? - For windows users, press `Ctrl + R` and type `cmd` and press enter. - For mac users, press `Cmd + Space` and type `terminal` and press enter. - For linux users, press `Ctrl + Alt + T`. 2. Do you have the correct version of `python` installed? Check with the following command: ```bash python --version ``` If you get an error, you need to install python. If your python version is lower than 3.10, you need to upgrade your python version. 3. Do you have `pip` installed properly? Check with the following command: ```bash pip --version ``` If you get an error, you need to install `pip`. 4. Did you encountered an error associated with installing the dependencies of this project? Try installing the dependencies manually using the following command: ```bash pip install pandas ``` If you get an error, the dependencies does not work on your machine. You will have to find your own way to install the dependencies. 5. If you are still having trouble, please feel free to open up an issue [here](https://github.com/FlysonBot/Mastermind/issues), and we will try to help you out. Or alternatively you can run the program in your [browser](https://colab.research.google.com/github/FlysonBot/Mastermind/blob/main/examples/mastermind_in_colab.ipynb) ## Contributing Contributions are welcome! Please open an issue or submit a pull request. All contributors must adhere to the [Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct](https://github.com/FlysonBot/Mastermind/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) to ensure a welcoming and inclusive environment for all contributors. To contribute to the code directly, you must also follow the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/FlysonBot/Mastermind/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) to ensure a smooth and efficient collaboration process. ## License Licensed under [MIT License](https://github.com/FlysonBot/Mastermind/blob/main/LICENSE) by [@FlysonBot](https://github.com/FlysonBot). ## Questions? If you have any questions, please feel free to leave them in the [Discussions](https://github.com/FlysonBot/Mastermind/discussions) or open up an [Issue](https://github.com/FlysonBot/Mastermind/issues).

inner_classes.name: mastermind.game.board.GameBoard.EmptyBoardError
documentation: Exception raised when trying to access the game board when it is empty.

Represents the game board for a Mastermind game.

The GameBoard class manages the game's guesses and feedbacks, allowing players to make guesses, retrieve past guesses and feedbacks, and clear the board.

Attributes:
    NUMBER_OF_COLORS (int): The number of colors available in the game.
    NUMBER_OF_DOTS (int): The number of dots (or pegs) in each guess.
    _number_of_guesses_made (int): The number of guesses made so far.
    _guesses (Stack): A stack of the guesses made so far.
    _feedbacks (Stack): A stack of the feedbacks received for the guesses made so far.

Raises:
    EmptyBoardError: If there are no guesses to retrieve or remove.

class_variables.name: mastermind.storage.persistent_cache.PersistentCacheManager._cache_directory
documentation: Directory to store cache files

Manages a persistent cache of Python objects on the file system.

The PersistentCacheManager class provides a simple interface for caching and retrieving data, using the pickle module to serialize and deserialize the objects.

The cache files are stored in the "data" directory, which is created automatically if it does not exist.

documentation: Dictionary to hold user data

class_variables.name: mastermind.storage.user_data.UserDataManager._file_path
documentation: Path to the user data file

Manages user data using a singleton pattern.

The UserDataManager class stores user data in a dictionary and persists it to a file on the file system using the pickle module.

The class is implemented as a singleton, ensuring that there is only one instance of the UserDataManager class.

Returns the item at the specified index from the top of the stack.

Args:
    index (int): The index of the item to retrieve, where 0 is the top of the stack.

Returns:
    Any: The item at the specified index.

Raises:
    IndexError: If the index is out of range.

methods.name: mastermind.utils.stack.Stack.clear
documentation: Clears all items from the stack.

methods.name: mastermind.utils.stack.Stack.as_list
documentation:
Returns the stack as a list, with the top item first.

Returns:
    list: The stack as a list.

methods.name: mastermind.utils.stack.Stack.__iter__
documentation: Returns an iterator over the stack.

A simple stack data structure implementation using the deque from the collections module.

Attributes:
    _stack (collections.deque): The internal deque used to store the stack elements.

modules.name: mastermind.utils.get_feedback
documentation: This module defines the generate_feedback function, which takes a guess and a secret code, and returns the number of black and white pegs.

functions.name: mastermind.utils.get_feedback.generate_feedback
documentation:
Generates feedback based on the guess and the secret code.

Args:
    guess (tuple): The player's guess.
    secret (tuple): The secret code.
    number_of_colors (int): The number of colors in the game.

Returns:
    list: A list containing the number of black and white pegs.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.models.numeric.NumberOfDots
documentation:
A ConstrainedInteger that validates the number of dots in the game.

The number of dots must be greater than or equal to 2.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.models.numeric.NumberOfColors
documentation:
A ConstrainedInteger that validates the number of colors in the game.

The number of colors must be greater than or equal to 2.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.models.numeric.NumberOfGuessesMade
documentation:
A ConstrainedInteger that validates the number of guesses made in the game.

The number of guesses must be greater than or equal to 0.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.models.numeric.MaximumAttempts
documentation:
A ConstrainedInteger that validates the maximum number of attempts allowed in the game.

The maximum number of attempts must be greater than or equal to 1.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.models.valid_combination.ValidCombination

methods.name: mastermind.validation.models.valid_combination.ValidCombination.__init__
documentation: Initializes the ValidCombination with the number of dots and colors.

methods.name: mastermind.validation.models.valid_combination.ValidCombination.validate_value
documentation:
Validates the given combination and returns the validated combination.

Args:
    value (Any): The combination to be validated.

Returns:
    Tuple[int, ...]: The validated combination.

Raises:
    TypeValidationError: If the combination is not a tuple or list of integers.
    RangeError: If the combination does not have the correct number of dots or the dot values are not within the valid range.

A ValidationModel that validates a combination of dots in a game.

Attributes:
    n_of_dots (int): The number of dots in the combination.
    n_of_colors (int): The number of colors available for the dots.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.models.valid_feedback.ValidFeedback

methods.name: mastermind.validation.models.valid_feedback.ValidFeedback.__init__
documentation: Initializes the ValidFeedback with the number of dots.

methods.name: mastermind.validation.models.valid_feedback.ValidFeedback.validate_arguments
documentation:
Validates the arguments used to initialize the ValidFeedback.

Raises:
    RangeError: If the number_of_dots parameter is not in valid range.

methods.name: mastermind.validation.models.valid_feedback.ValidFeedback.validate_value
documentation:
Validates the given feedback and returns the validated feedback.

Args:
    value (Any): The feedback to be validated.

Returns:
    Tuple[int, int]: The validated feedback.

Raises:
    InputConversionError: If the input cannot be converted a tuple of integers.
    TypeValidationError: If the feedback is not a tuple of two integers.
    RangeError: If the feedback values are not within the valid range.

A ValidationModel that validates the feedback for a game.

Attributes:
    number_of_dots (int): The number of dots in the combination.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.base.exceptions.ValidationError
documentation: The base class for all validation-related exceptions.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.base.exceptions.MissingParameterError
documentation: Raised when a required parameter is missing.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.base.exceptions.TypeValidationError
documentation: Raised when a value does not match the expected type.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.base.exceptions.InputConversionError
documentation: Raised when a value cannot be converted to the expected type.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.base.exceptions.RangeError
documentation: Raised when a value is outside the expected range.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.base.exceptions.InvalidModificationError
documentation: Raised when a modification to a validated value is invalid.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.models._game_utils.GameValidationUtils

methods.name: mastermind.validation.models._game_utils.GameValidationUtils.convert
documentation:
Converts a string representation of a combination or feedback into a tuple of integers.

Args:
    value (str): The string value to be converted.

Returns:
    Tuple[int, ...]: The converted tuple of integers.

Raises:
    InputConversionError: If the input string cannot be converted to a valid tuple of integers.

methods.name: mastermind.validation.models._game_utils.GameValidationUtils.validate_arguments
documentation:
Validates the arguments used to initialize the game-related validation models.

Args:
    instance: The instance of the game-related validation model.
Utility class for validating game-related inputs.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.base.semi_mutable.TrueFuse

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.semi_mutable.TrueFuse.validate_value
documentation:
Validates the initial value, ensuring it is a boolean.

Args:
    value (Any): The initial value.

Returns:
    bool: The validated value.

Raises:
    TypeValidationError: If the value is not a boolean.

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.semi_mutable.TrueFuse.validate_modifications
documentation:
Validates the modification to the value, ensuring it is set to True.

Args:
    new_value (Any): The new value to be set.

Raises:
    InvalidModificationError: If the new value is not True.
A StateValidator that can only be initialized to True or False, and can only be modified to True.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.base.base.Validator

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.base.Validator.validate_value
documentation:
Validates the given value and returns the validated value.

Args:
    value (Any): The value to be validated.

Returns:
    T: The validated value.

An abstract base class for all validators.

Defines the `validate_value` method that must be implemented by subclasses.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.base.base.ValidationModel

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.base.ValidationModel.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the validation model with the given keyword arguments.

Args:
    **kwargs: The keyword arguments to be used for initializing the validation model.

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.base.ValidationModel.validate_arguments
documentation:
Validates the arguments used to initialize the validation model.

This method must be implemented by subclasses to perform any necessary validation.

An abstract base class for validation models.

Provides a base implementation for initializing the validation model with keyword arguments and validating the arguments.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.base.base.StateValidator

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.base.StateValidator.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the state validator with the given value.

Args:
    value (Any): The initial value to be validated and stored.

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.base.StateValidator.validate_modifications
documentation:
Validates the modification of the value.

Args:
    new_value (Any): The new value to be validated.

Raises:
    ValidationError: If the new value is invalid.

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.base.StateValidator.value
documentation:
Returns the validated value.

Returns:
    T: The validated value.

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.base.StateValidator.value
documentation:
Sets the new value, validating the modification.

Args:
    new_value (Any): The new value to be set.

An abstract base class for state validators.

Provides a base implementation for managing the state of a validated value, including validation of modifications.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.base.validated_class.ValidatedClass

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.validated_class.ValidatedClass.__getattribute__
documentation:
Overrides the __getattribute__ method to automatically handle StateValidator attributes.

Args:
    name (str): The name of the attribute to be accessed.

Returns:
    Any: The value of the attribute, with any StateValidator instances unwrapped.

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.validated_class.ValidatedClass.__setattr__
documentation:
Overrides the __setattr__ method to automatically handle StateValidator attributes.

Args:
    name (str): The name of the attribute to be set.
    value (Any): The new value to be assigned to the attribute.

An abstract base class that provides automatic validation of class attributes.

When accessing or modifying an attribute of a ValidatedClass instance, if the attribute is a StateValidator, its value will be automatically validated and returned or updated.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.base.numeric.NumberRangeModel

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.numeric.NumberRangeModel.__init__
documentation: Initializes the NumberRangeModel with the specified range constraints.

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.numeric.NumberRangeModel.validate_arguments
documentation: Validates the range constraints provided to the NumberRangeModel.

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.numeric.NumberRangeModel.validate_value
documentation:
Validates the given value and returns the validated value.

Args:
    value (Number | str): The value to be validated.

Returns:
    Number: The validated value.

Raises:
    TypeValidationError: If the value is not a number or a string.
    InputConversionError: If the value cannot be converted to the expected numeric type.
    RangeError: If the value is outside the specified range.

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.numeric.NumberRangeModel.convert
documentation:
Converts the given string value to the expected numeric type.

Args:
    value (str): The string value to be converted.

Returns:
    Number: The converted numeric value.

Raises:
    InputConversionError: If the value cannot be converted to the expected numeric type.

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.numeric.NumberRangeModel.validate_range
documentation:
Validates that the given value is within the specified range.

Args:
    value (Number): The value to be validated.

Raises:
    RangeError: If the value is outside the specified range.

A ValidationModel that validates numeric values within a specified range.

Attributes:
    gt (Optional[Number]): The minimum value (exclusive).
    lt (Optional[Number]): The maximum value (exclusive).
    ge (Optional[Number]): The minimum value (inclusive).
    le (Optional[Number]): The maximum value (inclusive).

classes.name: mastermind.validation.base.numeric.ConstrainedInteger

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.numeric.ConstrainedInteger.convert
documentation:
Converts the given string value to an integer.

Args:
    value (str): The string value to be converted.

Returns:
    int: The converted integer value.

Raises:
    InputConversionError: If the value cannot be converted to an integer.
A NumberRangeModel that validates integer values within a specified range.

classes.name: mastermind.validation.base.numeric.ConstrainedFloat

methods.name: mastermind.validation.base.numeric.ConstrainedFloat.convert
documentation:
Converts the given string value to a float.

Args:
    value (str): The string value to be converted.

Returns:
    float: The converted float value.

Raises:
    InputConversionError: If the value cannot be converted to a float.
A NumberRangeModel that validates float values within a specified range.
